mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, leveraging advanced techniques like PagedAttention and continuous batching. Key trade-offs include the need for specific hardware configurations and potential complexity in setup.

steps.title: Add Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the vLLM Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.17.0

```

> This command adds the official vLLM Helm repository.

steps.title: Install Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
This step installs the necessary Custom Resource Definitions for vLLM to function correctly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.17.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRDs required for vLLM.

steps.title: Install vLLM with Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the vLLM application into your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.17.0

```

> This command installs vLLM in the 'vllm' namespace.

steps.title: Create Example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) to deploy a model using vLLM.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
EOF

```

> This command creates a sample vLLM resource for a GPT-3 model.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
EOF

steps.title: Verify vLLM Installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the vLLM deployment to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n vllm

```

> This command lists all pods in the vllm namespace to verify they are running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes the vLLM deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all related data and configurations.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command uninstalls the vLLM release.
> This command deletes the vllm namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. This step will upgrade vLLM to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n vllm -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.status.phase == "Running") | .metadata.name' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.17.0

kubectl get pods -n vllm

```

> This command drains all running pods in the vllm namespace.
> This command upgrades the vLLM release to the specified version.
> This command checks the status of the pods after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n vllm -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.status.phase == "Running") | .metadata.name' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm.io/v1: no matches for kind "VLLM" in version "vllm.io/v1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) have not been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Re-run the command to apply the CRDs: kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.17.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image ...'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified container image for vLLM is not available or the image tag is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the image tag in your Helm chart values and ensure it matches the available images.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is stuck in 'ContainerCreating' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur due to insufficient resources or issues with the underlying storage class.

**Fix:**
Check the resource requests and limits in your deployment and ensure your storage class is correctly configured.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the vLLM pod is crashing due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs for errors using kubectl logs <pod-name> and correct any configuration issues.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new decoding algorithms and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.16.0, ensure to review the new configuration options for decoding algorithms.

resolution.summary: You should see the vLLM pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n vllm'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
